calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Salvatore Timpano, Esq. of the Law Offices of Salvatore C. Timpano, T:415.992.5170, for plaintiff/opposing party;
Francis Torrence, Esq. of Wilson Elser, T:415.433.0990, for defendant/moving party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The deposition testimony and declaration of plaintiff Aleyda Catalan describing the feel and sight of the vomit and her experience of coming into contact with similar substances raise a reasonable inference, when viewed in the light most favorable to her, from which a trier of fact could find that the vomit had been on the platform long enough and was of such obvious nature to have been discovered by BART in the exercise of due care. (Catalan Depo. 31-33; Catalan Dec. par. 15). Ms. Catalan's declaration, which states in a formal way what she previously testified to in her deposition, is admissible lay expert testimony based on her direct observation and sensation of the vomit. The probity of her conclusions go to the weight of that evidence, not its admissibility, and is for the trier of fact to evaluate. There is a triable dispute whether the danger was so obvious and open to members of the public using the platform with due care as not to constitute a dangerous condition.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2017 Set For Trial On May-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
